code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "its runway is named {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the first runway is {obj} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the first runway is {obj} metres long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "the first runway surface is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the first runway number is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "the runway surface is {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "its runway is named {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {obj}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {obj}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {obj}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "has leader party {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "chief": "has chief {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "has native name {obj}",
        "class": "belongs to class {obj}",
        "division": "belongs to division {obj}",
        "order": "belongs to order {obj}",
        "species": "belongs to species {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "currency": "uses currency {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # Strip surrounding quotes from objects if present
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine parts into a fluent sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:

(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Use the subject of the first triple as the main entity
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    # Build attribute phrases for each triple
    phrases = []
    for t in triples:
        # Convert predicate to a more readable form
        pred_readable = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
        phrases.append(f"{pred_readable} {t.object}")
    # Assemble a single fluent sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        return f"{main_subject} has {phrases[0]}."
    else:
        all_but_last = ", ".join(phrases[:-1])
        return f"{main_subject} has {all_but_last}, and {phrases[-1]}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
